Container.Name: BeatsCheck
Container.Overview: Audio file integrity checker that decode-tests all audio files (FLAC, MP3, M4A, OGG, Opus, WAV, AAC, and more) using ffmpeg. Identifies corrupted files that cause playback errors.[br][br]The container scans on a schedule and stays running. Default: idle mode (no scanning until configured).[br][br]Settings are configured in /config/beatscheck.conf (auto-created on first run).[br]To review results: check /config/corrupt.txt[br]To delete corrupt files: docker exec -it BeatsCheck /app/delete.sh

Config.Name: Data
Config.Description: Mount your media/data folder — same path you use for Lidarr/Sonarr. Read-only for report mode, change to rw for move or delete.

Config.Name: Config
Config.Description: Persistent storage for logs, scan results, tracking data, and beatscheck.conf settings file.

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: Port for the optional web interface. Enable WebUI by setting webui = true in /config/beatscheck.conf. On first access, you will create login credentials via a setup wizard. To reset credentials: docker exec BeatsCheck reset-webui-password
